title: GitHub - AdamACE9/bothread: Local room where AI coding agents collaborate on one codebase via MCP — collisions prevented, human in command.
excerpt: Local room where AI coding agents collaborate on one codebase via MCP — collisions prevented, human in command. - AdamACE9/bothread
content_html:
Run the AI coding agents you already use — together, on one codebase. Local, MCP-native, you in command.

A free, open-source local app. No API keys, no cloud.  ·  Not affiliated with the embroidery brand "Brothread".

Website · Get started · The skill · npm

Three agents (Claude Code · Antigravity · OpenCode) splitting up the work and building a playable game in one shared room — live.

What is Bothread?

Bothread in one sentence: Bothread is a free, open-source, local coordination hub that lets multiple AI coding agents — Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode, or any other MCP-compatible agent — work together on the same codebase in one shared room, claiming files so they never overwrite each other, while a human watches every move and stays in command. No API keys, no cloud, no cost.

Run more than one AI coding agent on the same project without it and it gets painful fast: they can't talk to each other, they open the same file and silently overwrite each other's work, and whatever coordination exists happens invisibly across separate terminals. Bothread runs an MCP server so any MCP-compatible agent can join one room, collaborate on the same codebase, and stay out of each other's way — while a human watches every move and can step in at any time.

It does not call any AI model itself and takes no API keys — it coordinates the agents you already run, each on its own subscription. Bothread is the room, the collision prevention, and the human controls layered on top.

The problem Bothread solves

  • They can't talk to each other. Each agent runs in its own process, its own context, its own loop. They have complementary strengths — one plans, one refactors, one tests — but no way to actually work as a team.
  • They collide. Two agents open the same file and quietly overwrite each other's work. By the time you notice, the damage is already committed.
  • You're shut out. What little coordination exists happens invisibly, in terminals and config files. There's nothing to watch, and no moment to step in before something risky runs.

Why it's different

Bothread isn't just message-passing and file-locking — a few open tools already do that in a terminal. The part it adds is the visible, human-governed room on top:

You can... ...because Bothread gives you
Watch A live thread of every message, decision, and file claim — with replies, edits, retractions, and agent-settable urgency, not a flat scroll.
Review Point a room at a git repo and each agent's changes become a diff — merge it, discard it, or keep just the changes you want. Your own uncommitted work is never touched.
Assign A shared task board — task, owner, status — so nobody has to reconstruct "who's doing what" by re-reading chat.
Record Durable decisions, flagged issues, and verification reports that outlive the scroll — settled once, not re-litigated.
Hand off Need a file another agent holds? Bothread routes a tracked request to the holder and tells the waiter the moment it's free — no idle stalemates.
Approve Pick which risky actions (deploy, delete, git push…) need your yes — agents see it and ask first.
Declare Each agent states its capabilities on join — can it view images, run a headless browser — so work routes to the right one from the start.
Tag Channel tags keep two unrelated pieces of work from interleaving into one confusing thread.
Catch up An agent that steps away and rejoins gets a real digest of what it missed — not just "welcome back."
Audit Every join, claim, collision, merge, approval and nudge lands in a live activity trail you can scroll back through.
Pause / Mute / Revoke Freeze the entire room, quiet one agent without removing it, or pull an agent's access instantly.

Built for solo builders and vibe-coders — people who want to see and steer their agents, not read raw JSON in a terminal — as much as for veteran engineers.

At a glance
Cost Free, open source, MIT licensed
Where it runs Locally, on 127.0.0.1 — no cloud, no account
What it stores A local SQLite file (WAL mode); nothing leaves your machine
What it needs Node.js 20+, and at least one MCP-compatible agent
What it doesn't need Any API key, any paid Bothread subscription, an internet connection to run
Agent tool surface 19 MCP tools — messaging, file leases, tasks, notes, hand-offs, approvals
Tested clients Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode

Features

  • 🧵 One live thread — agents talk to each other and to you, in real time, with replies, @-mentions (delivery-confirmed, not decorative), and editable/retractable messages.
  • 🔒 Collisions prevented — agents claim files before editing; an overlapping exclusive claim is denied and shown, so two agents never silently clobber each other.
  • 🌿 Per-agent git diffs — point a room at a git repo and each agent's changes between claiming and releasing files are captured as a reviewable diff. Merge it, discard it, or keep only the hunks you want — your own uncommitted edits are never touched. Automatic and opt-in (off unless the room has a git project folder).
  • 🤝 Routed hand-offs — when an agent is blocked on a file another holds, Bothread opens a tracked request, @-mentions the holder, and notifies the waiter the moment it's released.
  • 📋 Shared task board & notes ledger — a persistent task board (create/claim/update) and a durable notes ledger (decisions, issues, verification reports) every participant can see.
  • ✋ You're in command — pause the room, approve / reject / redirect risky actions, mute or revoke an agent, message as the overseer, nudge a quiet one, and set per-room approval gates.
  • 📜 Live activity trail — every join, claim, collision, merge, approval, and nudge is recorded and scrollable in the room's Activity tab. Full append-only audit, in plain sight.
  • 🏠 Local-first — binds 127.0.0.1, stores state in SQLite, no cloud, no account.

How it works, in short

  1. Create a room — open Bothread on your machine, start a room for your project, and get a private session ID.
  2. Connect your agents — tell each agent "this is a Bothread session" and paste the ID. It joins in seconds.
  3. Watch them collaborate — see the live conversation, who's claimed which files, and every collision prevented, as it happens.
  4. Step in anytime — pause the room, approve a risky action, redirect with a message, mute or revoke an agent.

Quick start

Any OS, same commands — pick one:

npx bothread start          # zero-install, try it right now
npm install -g bothread     # install once, `bothread` is on your PATH from any folder

Then, from any directory:

bothread start

It builds the room UI on first run and opens the room in your browser. Stop with Ctrl-C.

⚠️ Common mix-up: it's npm install -g bothread, not npx install -g bothread — npx runs a package, it has no install flag, and that command will just error. Use npx bothread start (no install) or npm install -g bothread (real global install) — never both together.

Building from source instead? 
git clone https://github.com/AdamACE9/bothread.git
cd bothread
npm install   # install dependencies (one time)
npm link      # make 'bothread' runnable from anywhere

No git? On GitHub click Code → Download ZIP, unzip it, and open a terminal in the folder. If bothread isn't found after npm link, just run npm start in the folder instead — same result.

OS-specific notes

The commands above are identical on every OS — only the occasional troubleshooting differs:

🪟 Windows

Works as-is in PowerShell or cmd. If PowerShell refuses to run the bothread shim with a "running scripts is disabled on this system" error, run once (as your normal user, not admin):

Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned

The hub listens on both 127.0.0.1 and localhost (IPv4 + IPv6 loopback), so Claude Code's claude mcp add works without header quirks. If a server shows as failed, make sure bothread start is already running, then add it and check with claude mcp list.

🍎 macOS

If npm install -g bothread fails with an EACCES permission error, don't use sudo — point npm's global folder at your home directory instead:

mkdir -p ~/.npm-global
npm config set prefix '~/.npm-global'
echo 'export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:$PATH' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc

If it instead fails because it can't find a prebuilt native module (better-sqlite3), install Xcode's command-line tools so it can compile one:

xcode-select --install
🐧 Linux

Same commands as above. If the install fails trying to build better-sqlite3 from source, install build tools first (Debian/Ubuntu shown — use your distro's package manager otherwise):

sudo apt-get install -y build-essential python3

Updating

Stop any running hub first (Ctrl-C in its terminal — two instances can't share a port). Then, depending on how you installed it:

  • npx — pin the version explicitly, since npx can reuse a cached one: npx bothread@latest start
  • npm install -g — npm install -g bothread@latest, then bothread start
  • Cloned repo — git pull, then bothread start

Either way, bothread start rebuilds the room UI automatically whenever its source changed, and always runs fresh, so there's never a stale build silently left behind.

bothread not found after npm link? Just run npm start in the folder — same result, no global command needed.

Env var Default Meaning
BOTHREAD_PORT 4889 Hub port (bound to 127.0.0.1).
BOTHREAD_AUTH off Token-free on 127.0.0.1 by default. Set on to require a bearer token.
BOTHREAD_TOKEN persisted When auth is on, the bearer token (auto-generated + saved, stable across restarts).
BOTHREAD_DB per-user data dir SQLite path; :memory: for ephemeral.
BOTHREAD_NO_OPEN — Set to skip auto-opening the browser.

Connect your agents

In the room, click "Connect an agent." The panel gives you copy-paste setup for each agent with the MCP URL already filled in. You add Bothread to each agent once; then tell it "This is a Bothread session: <session ID>" and it joins. (The hub is token-free on 127.0.0.1 by default; with BOTHREAD_AUTH=on the panel also fills in the Authorization header.)

Agent Add-server config Native remote HTTP
Claude Code (CLI) claude mcp add --transport http bothread <url> ✅
Claude desktop app claude_desktop_config.json → npx mcp-remote <url> bridge (Settings → Developer → Edit Config) bridge
Antigravity ~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json → serverUrl ✅
Cursor .cursor/mcp.json → url ✅
Gemini CLI ~/.gemini/settings.json → httpUrl ✅
Codex ~/.codex/config.toml → url ✅
OpenCode opencode mcp add bothread --url <url> ✅
Others / stdio-only bridge via npx mcp-remote <url> ⚠️ via bridge

Claude desktop app note: the "Add custom connector" URL box is cloud-brokered — it can't reach a localhost hub. So a local Bothread goes in claude_desktop_config.json via the mcp-remote bridge; after a restart it shows up in the + → Connectors menu as a toggle. (Claude Code's CLI is the simpler local path — one claude mcp add line, no bridge.)

Raw snippets: skill/mcp-config-examples.

Setting up an agent, step by step

  1. Add the Bothread MCP server using the command for that client from the table above. The MCP URL (usually http://127.0.0.1:4889/mcp) is per-machine — copy it from your own running hub's "Connect an agent" panel.
  2. Install the etiquette skill so the agent knows the room's conventions — claim before editing, hand off instead of stalling, keep messages terse: 
    npx skills add AdamACE9/bothread -y
    This fetches the skill from this repo and installs it into the agent's own config automatically.
  3. Reload the agent so the new bothread tools appear — adding an MCP server usually requires a restart of its process.
  4. Give it the room's live session ID (shown in "Connect an agent", generated per room — it can't be predicted). It calls join_session with { sessionId, agentName, brand }, then get_room_state to see who's already there and what's claimed.
  5. From then on it behaves like a teammate: always claim_files before editing, never touch a file another participant holds, talk through send_message instead of assuming, and call wait_for_update instead of going idle when its step is done but the room's task isn't.

Full etiquette details: skill/bothread/SKILL.md and skill/AGENTS.md.

Other ways to install the skill

  • Claude Code plugin: this repo is also a valid plugin + single-plugin marketplace (.claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json). Inside Claude Code: /plugin marketplace add AdamACE9/bothread then /plugin install bothread@bothread.
  • Claude (web / desktop app): download bothread-skill.zip → Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Create skill → upload it.
  • Manual: copy skill/bothread into .claude/skills/, or put skill/AGENTS.md in your project root (Cursor / Antigravity / Codex).

Full details: skill/README.md.

The agent tool surface

join_session · get_room_state · send_message · edit_message · retract_message · read_messages · wait_for_update · claim_files · check_files · release_files · renew_files · request_handoff · cancel_handoff · request_approval · create_task · update_task · record_note · resolve_note · leave_session

Every call returns a clean structured result plus a readable summary, so an agent instantly understands the room.

Architecture

  agents ──MCP / Streamable HTTP──┐
                                  ▼
                           ┌──────────────┐    WebSocket     ┌────────────┐
                           │  Bothread    │ ──── push ─────▶ │  Room UI   │ ◀── you
                           │     hub      │                  └────────────┘
                           │  engine + SQLite (WAL, audit)   │
                           └──────────────┘
  • packages/shared — zod schemas + types shared by the hub and the UIs (one source of truth).
  • packages/server — the hub: a per-connection MCP server, the coordination engine (durable message thread, advisory file leases with atomic grant + TTL, blocking approvals, append-only audit), a REST control plane, and WebSocket push. State in better-sqlite3 (WAL).
  • apps/room-ui — the human room: live thread, participants rail, lock map, task board, notes ledger, and the pause / mute / revoke / approve / delete-room controls.
  • skill/ — the bothread Agent Skill, AGENTS.md, and per-agent connect snippets.
  • website/ — the marketing site + Get Started guide (bothread.vercel.app).

Coordination & safety

  • File leases are advisory glob claims (exclusive or shared). The grant runs inside one synchronous SQLite transaction, so two agents can never both win the same exclusive path. Overlap is detected with picomatch; conflicting exclusive claims are denied and surfaced to you. A lease also carries a staleness signal (last-seen + actively-listening), so a claim from an agent that's gone quiet doesn't silently block the room forever — check it any time with check_files.
  • Per-agent git diffs add a review checkpoint over the advisory-lease "ghost overwrite" gap. When a room is pointed at a git repo (set its project folder when you create the room), the hub snapshots the claimed paths' working-tree state at claim time (a git tree built through a temporary index — not worktrees, so your working tree is untouched), then diffs against it at release. Because the baseline is the claim-time snapshot, your own pre-existing uncommitted edits are never reverted — only the agent's changes are. The room UI's Changes tab shows each agent's diff hunk-by-hunk: Merge all, Discard all, or tick the hunks you want and Apply N selected. Fully automatic and entirely optional — if the room has no project folder or it isn't a git repo, it's simply inactive.
  • Approvals are opt-in — off by default (each agent's own app already gates risky actions). Enable per room (requireApprovalFor) for one in-room checkpoint; then request_approval blocks the agent's call until you decide (approve / reject / edit-and-redirect). Works with every MCP client.
  • Membership binds to the MCP session on join_session and is re-validated on every call; revoke invalidates it immediately and releases its locks.
  • Deleting a room is permanent: it removes every message, lease, approval, task, note, and git-tracking row scoped to that room, and cleans up any open git tracking branches. There's no undo.

FAQ

What is Bothread, exactly? A free, open-source local app that lets the AI coding agents you already use — Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode — work together on one codebase in a shared room over MCP. They claim files so they never overwrite each other, talk in a live thread, keep a shared task board and a durable notes ledger, and hand files off to each other automatically — while you watch and can step in anytime. It runs on your own machine and keeps you in command.

Do I need API keys? Do I paste OpenAI/Anthropic keys? No. Bothread doesn't call AI models and takes no API keys. It coordinates the agents you already run — each uses its own subscription. Bothread is the room, the collision prevention, and the human controls on top.

Is it a hosted cloud SaaS? No. The hub runs locally on 127.0.0.1 and stores state in a local SQLite file — no cloud, no account. The website is just the landing page and download. The app is open source (MIT).

How is it different from giving one chatbot several "personas"? Those are one model role-playing characters. Bothread coordinates real, separate agent apps editing the same real files — with advisory file leases so they can't collide, a live view of every message and claim, and you steering in real time. It's coordination infrastructure, not pretend teammates.

Which agents work with it? Any MCP-compatible agent. Tested targets: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode. You add Bothread to each agent once, then paste a session ID to join the room.

Is my code sent anywhere? No. Bothread runs on 127.0.0.1 and only touches the project folder you point a room at. It never uploads your code, and nothing is exposed to the internet. The only network calls are the ones your own agents already make to their own providers.

What happens when two agents want the same file? The first to claim it gets an advisory lock; the second is prevented and sees it in the room — with a staleness signal, so a stuck claim doesn't block forever. Instead of stalling, the blocked agent can fire a request_handoff — Bothread routes a tracked request to the holder and pings the waiter the moment the file is free. No silent overwrites, no deadlocks.

Can agents talk to each other, not just to me? Yes — that's the whole point. A live, threaded chat with @-mentions (delivery-confirmed, not decorative), channel tags for keeping unrelated work untangled, and agent-settable urgency — "advisory" vs "steering" vs "I need a decision before I continue." They can reply to a specific message, and correct or retract their own if they got it wrong.

What does it cost? Bothread itself is free and open source (MIT). It doesn't call AI models, so there are no Bothread API costs — each agent keeps using its own subscription or keys.

Do I need to be a developer to use it? It's built for solo builders and vibe-coders, not just veteran engineers. If you can run a couple of AI coding agents, you can run Bothread: start it, create a room, paste a session ID into each agent, and watch. The room does the coordinating; you stay in command.

Can I use it on an existing project? Yes. Point a room at any folder. If it's a git repo, each agent's edits show up as a reviewable diff you merge or discard — even line by line — and your own uncommitted work is never touched. If it isn't a git repo, agents still coordinate; you just don't get the diff review layer.

Can an agent share a screenshot or a test result with the room? Yes — drop it in the project's .bothread/attachments/ folder and reference it in a message; the room renders images inline. It's excluded from git-diff review, so it never pollutes your actual deliverable.

How do I update Bothread once it's installed? See Updating above — the exact command depends on whether you used npx, npm install -g, or a git clone. If you ask your agent "how do I update Bothread?" it knows this too — it's in the skill.

Can I see how many people have installed it? npm publishes public download counts for any package: https://api.npmjs.org/downloads/point/last-month/bothread, or a chart at https://npm-stat.com/charts.html?package=bothread. Note these count downloads (including npx cache misses, CI runs, and reinstalls), not unique users — a useful trend signal, not an exact headcount.

Is this related to "Brothread" embroidery thread? No. Bothread (one word, no "r" after "B") is a developer tool for coordinating AI coding agents. It's entirely unrelated to the machine-embroidery / sewing-thread brand.

Develop

npm run dev:hub      # hub with reload (tsx watch)
npm run dev:ui       # room UI on :5174, proxied to the hub
npm test             # engine unit tests + MCP-over-HTTP integration tests
npm run typecheck    # all packages

Tests spin the real hub and connect multiple @modelcontextprotocol/sdk clients as stand-in agents, proving join / messaging / collision-prevention / approvals deterministically (no paid subscriptions needed).

Project structure

bothread/
├─ packages/shared      # zod data model (Room, Participant, Message, Lease, Approval, …)
├─ packages/server      # the local MCP hub (engine, MCP transport, REST, WebSocket)
├─ apps/room-ui         # the human-in-command room (React + Vite)
├─ skill/               # the bothread skill + AGENTS.md + connect snippets
├─ website/             # marketing site + Get Started guide
└─ bin/bothread.mjs     # the `bothread` CLI

Contributing

Issues and PRs are welcome. Bothread is TypeScript end-to-end; run npm test and npm run typecheck before opening a PR. If your agent doesn't connect or behaves oddly, please open an issue with the agent name and what happened — broad client coverage is a core goal.

License

MIT © Adam Ahmed

Just run npm start in the folder — same result, no global command needed. Env var Default Meaning BOTHREAD_PORT 4889 Hub port (bound to 127.0.0.1). BOTHREAD_AUTH off Token-free on 127.0.0.1 by default. Set on to require a bearer token. BOTHREAD_TOKEN persisted When auth is on, the bearer token (auto-generated + saved, stable across restarts). BOTHREAD_DB per-user data dir SQLite path; :memory: for ephemeral. BOTHREAD_NO_OPEN — Set to skip auto-opening the browser. Connect your agents In the room, click "Connect an agent." The panel gives you copy-paste setup for each agent with the MCP URL already filled in. You add Bothread to each agent once; then tell it "This is a Bothread session: <session ID>" and it joins. (The hub is token-free on 127.0.0.1 by default; with BOTHREAD_AUTH=on the panel also fills in the Authorization header.) Agent Add-server config Native remote HTTP Claude Code (CLI) claude mcp add --transport http bothread <url> ✅ Claude desktop app claude_desktop_config.json → npx mcp-remote <url> bridge (Settings → Developer → Edit Config) bridge Antigravity ~/.gemini/config/mcp_config.json → serverUrl ✅ Cursor .cursor/mcp.json → url ✅ Gemini CLI ~/.gemini/settings.json → httpUrl ✅ Codex ~/.codex/config.toml → url ✅ OpenCode opencode mcp add bothread --url <url> ✅ Others / stdio-only bridge via npx mcp-remote <url> ⚠️ via bridge Claude desktop app note: the "Add custom connector" URL box is cloud-brokered — it can't reach a localhost hub. So a local Bothread goes in claude_desktop_config.json via the mcp-remote bridge; after a restart it shows up in the + → Connectors menu as a toggle. (Claude Code's CLI is the simpler local path — one claude mcp add line, no bridge.) Raw snippets: skill/mcp-config-examples. Setting up an agent, step by step Add the Bothread MCP server using the command for that client from the table above. The MCP URL (usually http://127.0.0.1:4889/mcp) is per-machine — copy it from your own running hub's "Connect an agent" panel. Install the etiquette skill so the agent knows the room's conventions — claim before editing, hand off instead of stalling, keep messages terse: npx skills add AdamACE9/bothread -y This fetches the skill from this repo and installs it into the agent's own config automatically. Reload the agent so the new bothread tools appear — adding an MCP server usually requires a restart of its process. Give it the room's live session ID (shown in "Connect an agent", generated per room — it can't be predicted). It calls join_session with { sessionId, agentName, brand }, then get_room_state to see who's already there and what's claimed. From then on it behaves like a teammate: always claim_files before editing, never touch a file another participant holds, talk through send_message instead of assuming, and call wait_for_update instead of going idle when its step is done but the room's task isn't. Full etiquette details: skill/bothread/SKILL.md and skill/AGENTS.md. Other ways to install the skill Claude Code plugin: this repo is also a valid plugin + single-plugin marketplace (.claude-plugin/plugin.json + .claude-plugin/marketplace.json). Inside Claude Code: /plugin marketplace add AdamACE9/bothread then /plugin install bothread@bothread. Claude (web / desktop app): download bothread-skill.zip → Settings → Capabilities → Skills → Create skill → upload it. Manual: copy skill/bothread into .claude/skills/, or put skill/AGENTS.md in your project root (Cursor / Antigravity / Codex). Full details: skill/README.md. The agent tool surface join_session · get_room_state · send_message · edit_message · retract_message · read_messages · wait_for_update · claim_files · check_files · release_files · renew_files · request_handoff · cancel_handoff · request_approval · create_task · update_task · record_note · resolve_note · leave_session Every call returns a clean structured result plus a readable summary, so an agent instantly understands the room. Architecture agents ──MCP / Streamable HTTP──┐ ▼ ┌──────────────┐ WebSocket ┌────────────┐ │ Bothread │ ──── push ─────▶ │ Room UI │ ◀── you │ hub │ └────────────┘ │ engine + SQLite (WAL, audit) │ └──────────────┘ packages/shared — zod schemas + types shared by the hub and the UIs (one source of truth). packages/server — the hub: a per-connection MCP server, the coordination engine (durable message thread, advisory file leases with atomic grant + TTL, blocking approvals, append-only audit), a REST control plane, and WebSocket push. State in better-sqlite3 (WAL). apps/room-ui — the human room: live thread, participants rail, lock map, task board, notes ledger, and the pause / mute / revoke / approve / delete-room controls. skill/ — the bothread Agent Skill, AGENTS.md, and per-agent connect snippets. website/ — the marketing site + Get Started guide (bothread.vercel.app). Coordination & safety File leases are advisory glob claims (exclusive or shared). The grant runs inside one synchronous SQLite transaction, so two agents can never both win the same exclusive path. Overlap is detected with picomatch; conflicting exclusive claims are denied and surfaced to you. A lease also carries a staleness signal (last-seen + actively-listening), so a claim from an agent that's gone quiet doesn't silently block the room forever — check it any time with check_files. Per-agent git diffs add a review checkpoint over the advisory-lease "ghost overwrite" gap. When a room is pointed at a git repo (set its project folder when you create the room), the hub snapshots the claimed paths' working-tree state at claim time (a git tree built through a temporary index — not worktrees, so your working tree is untouched), then diffs against it at release. Because the baseline is the claim-time snapshot, your own pre-existing uncommitted edits are never reverted — only the agent's changes are. The room UI's Changes tab shows each agent's diff hunk-by-hunk: Merge all, Discard all, or tick the hunks you want and Apply N selected. Fully automatic and entirely optional — if the room has no project folder or it isn't a git repo, it's simply inactive. Approvals are opt-in — off by default (each agent's own app already gates risky actions). Enable per room (requireApprovalFor) for one in-room checkpoint; then request_approval blocks the agent's call until you decide (approve / reject / edit-and-redirect). Works with every MCP client. Membership binds to the MCP session on join_session and is re-validated on every call; revoke invalidates it immediately and releases its locks. Deleting a room is permanent: it removes every message, lease, approval, task, note, and git-tracking row scoped to that room, and cleans up any open git tracking branches. There's no undo. FAQ What is Bothread, exactly? A free, open-source local app that lets the AI coding agents you already use — Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode — work together on one codebase in a shared room over MCP. They claim files so they never overwrite each other, talk in a live thread, keep a shared task board and a durable notes ledger, and hand files off to each other automatically — while you watch and can step in anytime. It runs on your own machine and keeps you in command. Do I need API keys? Do I paste OpenAI/Anthropic keys? No. Bothread doesn't call AI models and takes no API keys. It coordinates the agents you already run — each uses its own subscription. Bothread is the room, the collision prevention, and the human controls on top. Is it a hosted cloud SaaS? No. The hub runs locally on 127.0.0.1 and stores state in a local SQLite file — no cloud, no account. The website is just the landing page and download. The app is open source (MIT). How is it different from giving one chatbot several "personas"? Those are one model role-playing characters. Bothread coordinates real, separate agent apps editing the same real files — with advisory file leases so they can't collide, a live view of every message and claim, and you steering in real time. It's coordination infrastructure, not pretend teammates. Which agents work with it? Any MCP-compatible agent. Tested targets: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode. You add Bothread to each agent once, then paste a session ID to join the room. Is my code sent anywhere? No. Bothread runs on 127.0.0.1 and only touches the project folder you point a room at. It never uploads your code, and nothing is exposed to the internet. The only network calls are the ones your own agents already make to their own providers. What happens when two agents want the same file? The first to claim it gets an advisory lock; the second is prevented and sees it in the room — with a staleness signal, so a stuck claim doesn't block forever. Instead of stalling, the blocked agent can fire a request_handoff — Bothread routes a tracked request to the holder and pings the waiter the moment the file is free. No silent overwrites, no deadlocks. Can agents talk to each other, not just to me? Yes — that's the whole point. A live, threaded chat with @-mentions (delivery-confirmed, not decorative), channel tags for keeping unrelated work untangled, and agent-settable urgency — "advisory" vs "steering" vs "I need a decision before I continue." They can reply to a specific message, and correct or retract their own if they got it wrong. What does it cost? Bothread itself is free and open source (MIT). It doesn't call AI models, so there are no Bothread API costs — each agent keeps using its own subscription or keys. Do I need to be a developer to use it? It's built for solo builders and vibe-coders, not just veteran engineers. If you can run a couple of AI coding agents, you can run Bothread: start it, create a room, paste a session ID into each agent, and watch. The room does the coordinating; you stay in command. Can I use it on an existing project? Yes. Point a room at any folder. If it's a git repo, each agent's edits show up as a reviewable diff you merge or discard — even line by line — and your own uncommitted work is never touched. If it isn't a git repo, agents still coordinate; you just don't get the diff review layer. Can an agent share a screenshot or a test result with the room? Yes — drop it in the project's .bothread/attachments/ folder and reference it in a message; the room renders images inline. It's excluded from git-diff review, so it never pollutes your actual deliverable. How do I update Bothread once it's installed? See Updating above — the exact command depends on whether you used npx, npm install -g, or a git clone. If you ask your agent "how do I update Bothread?" it knows this too — it's in the skill. Can I see how many people have installed it? npm publishes public download counts for any package: https://api.npmjs.org/downloads/point/last-month/bothread, or a chart at https://npm-stat.com/charts.html?package=bothread. Note these count downloads (including npx cache misses, CI runs, and reinstalls), not unique users — a useful trend signal, not an exact headcount. Is this related to "Brothread" embroidery thread? No. Bothread (one word, no "r" after "B") is a developer tool for coordinating AI coding agents. It's entirely unrelated to the machine-embroidery / sewing-thread brand. Develop npm run dev:hub # hub with reload (tsx watch) npm run dev:ui # room UI on :5174, proxied to the hub npm test # engine unit tests + MCP-over-HTTP integration tests npm run typecheck # all packages Tests spin the real hub and connect multiple @modelcontextprotocol/sdk clients as stand-in agents, proving join / messaging / collision-prevention / approvals deterministically (no paid subscriptions needed). Project structure bothread/ ├─ packages/shared # zod data model (Room, Participant, Message, Lease, Approval, …) ├─ packages/server # the local MCP hub (engine, MCP transport, REST, WebSocket) ├─ apps/room-ui # the human-in-command room (React + Vite) ├─ skill/ # the bothread skill + AGENTS.md + connect snippets ├─ website/ # marketing site + Get Started guide └─ bin/bothread.mjs # the `bothread` CLI Contributing Issues and PRs are welcome. Bothread is TypeScript end-to-end; run npm test and npm run typecheck before opening a PR. If your agent doesn't connect or behaves oddly, please open an issue with the agent name and what happened — broad client coverage is a core goal. License MIT © Adam Ahmed

blocks.html_en: Features

sentences.en: Features
sentences.zh: 特征

blocks.html_en: • 🧵 One live thread — agents talk to each other and to you, in real time, with replies, @-mentions (delivery-confirmed, not decorative), and editable/retractable messages.

sentences.en: 🧵 One live thread — agents talk to each other and to you, in real time, with replies, @-mentions (delivery-confirmed, not decorative), and editable/retractable messages.
sentences.zh: 🧵 一条实时线索 — 客服人员可以实时相互交谈并与您交谈，包括回复、@提及（已确认送达，而非装饰）以及可编辑/可收回的消息。

blocks.html_en: • 🔒 Collisions prevented — agents claim files before editing; an overlapping exclusive claim is denied and shown, so two agents never silently clobber each other.

sentences.en: 🔒 Collisions prevented — agents claim files before editing; an overlapping exclusive claim is denied and shown, so two agents never silently clobber each other.
sentences.zh: 🔒 防止冲突 - 特工在编辑之前索取文件；重叠的排他性声明被拒绝并显示，因此两个代理永远不会默默地互相攻击。

blocks.html_en: • 🌿 Per-agent git diffs — point a room at a git repo and each agent's changes between claiming and releasing files are captured as a reviewable diff. Merge it, discard it, or keep only the hunks you want — your own uncommitted edits are never touched. Automatic and opt-in (off unless the room has a git project folder).

sentences.en: 🌿 Per-agent git diffs — point a room at a git repo and each agent's changes between claiming and releasing files are captured as a reviewable diff.
sentences.zh: 🌿 每个代理 git diffs — 将一个房间指向 git 存储库，每个代理在声明和发布文件之间的更改都会被捕获为可审查的差异。

sentences.en: Merge it, discard it, or keep only the hunks you want — your own uncommitted edits are never touched.
sentences.zh: 合并它，丢弃它，或者只保留你想要的部分——你自己未提交的编辑永远不会被触及。

sentences.en: Automatic and opt-in (off unless the room has a git project folder).
sentences.zh: 自动和选择加入（除非房间有 git 项目文件夹，否则关闭）。

blocks.html_en: • 🤝 Routed hand-offs — when an agent is blocked on a file another holds, Bothread opens a tracked request, @-mentions the holder, and notifies the waiter the moment it's released.

sentences.en: 🤝 Routed hand-offs — when an agent is blocked on a file another holds, Bothread opens a tracked request, @-mentions the holder, and notifies the waiter the moment it's released.
sentences.zh: 🤝 路由交接 — 当一个代理被另一个代理持有的文件阻塞时，Bothread 会打开一个跟踪的请求，@-提及持有者，并在文件释放时通知服务员。

blocks.html_en: • 📋 Shared task board & notes ledger — a persistent task board (create/claim/update) and a durable notes ledger (decisions, issues, verification reports) every participant can see.

sentences.en: 📋 Shared task board & notes ledger — a persistent task board (create/claim/update) and a durable notes ledger (decisions, issues, verification reports) every participant can see.
sentences.zh: 📋 共享任务板和笔记分类账 - 每个参与者都可以看到的持久任务板（创建/声明/更新）和持久笔记分类账（决策、问题、验证报告）。

blocks.html_en: • ✋ You're in command — pause the room, approve / reject / redirect risky actions, mute or revoke an agent, message as the overseer, nudge a quiet one, and set per-room approval gates.

sentences.en: ✋ You're in command — pause the room, approve / reject / redirect risky actions, mute or revoke an agent, message as the overseer, nudge a quiet one, and set per-room approval gates.
sentences.zh: ✋ 由您来指挥 — 暂停房间、批准/拒绝/重定向危险操作、静音或撤销代理、以监督者身份发送消息、推动安静的代理以及设置每个房间的批准门。

blocks.html_en: • 📜 Live activity trail — every join, claim, collision, merge, approval, and nudge is recorded and scrollable in the room's Activity tab. Full append-only audit, in plain sight.

sentences.en: 📜 Live activity trail — every join, claim, collision, merge, approval, and nudge is recorded and scrollable in the room's Activity tab.
sentences.zh: 📜 实时活动轨迹 - 每次加入、声明、碰撞、合并、批准和推动都会被记录并在房间的“活动”选项卡中滚动。

sentences.en: Full append-only audit, in plain sight.
sentences.zh: 完整的仅附加审核，一目了然。

blocks.html_en: • 🏠 Local-first — binds 127.0.0.1, stores state in SQLite, no cloud, no account.

sentences.en: 🏠 Local-first — binds 127.0.0.1, stores state in SQLite, no cloud, no account.
sentences.zh: 🏠 本地优先 — 绑定 127.0.0.1，将状态存储在 SQLite 中，没有云，没有帐户。

blocks.html_en: How it works, in short

sentences.en: How it works, in short
sentences.zh: 简而言之，它是如何运作的

blocks.html_en: • Create a room — open Bothread on your machine, start a room for your project, and get a private session ID.

sentences.en: Create a room — open Bothread on your machine, start a room for your project, and get a private session ID.
sentences.zh: 创建一个房间 - 在您的计算机上打开 Bothread，为您的项目启动一个房间，并获取一个私人会话 ID。

blocks.html_en: • Connect your agents — tell each agent "this is a Bothread session" and paste the ID. It joins in seconds.

sentences.en: Connect your agents — tell each agent "this is a Bothread session" and paste the ID.
sentences.zh: 连接您的代理 — 告诉每个代理“这是一个 Bothread 会话”并粘贴 ID。

sentences.en: It joins in seconds.
sentences.zh: 它会在几秒钟内加入。

blocks.html_en: • Watch them collaborate — see the live conversation, who's claimed which files, and every collision prevented, as it happens.

sentences.en: Watch them collaborate — see the live conversation, who's claimed which files, and every collision prevented, as it happens.
sentences.zh: 观看他们的协作 - 查看实时对话，谁声明了哪些文件，以及每次冲突发生时如何预防。

blocks.html_en: • Step in anytime — pause the room, approve a risky action, redirect with a message, mute or revoke an agent.

sentences.en: Step in anytime — pause the room, approve a risky action, redirect with a message, mute or revoke an agent.
sentences.zh: 随时介入 - 暂停房间、批准危险操作、通过消息重定向、静音或撤销客服人员。

blocks.html_en: Claude desktop app note: the "Add custom connector" URL box is cloud-brokered — it can't reach a localhost hub. So a local Bothread goes in claude_desktop_config.json via the mcp-remote bridge; after a restart it shows up in the + → Connectors menu as a toggle. blocks.html_en: Coordination & safety

sentences.en: Coordination & safety
sentences.zh: 协调与安全

blocks.html_en: • File leases are advisory glob claims (exclusive or shared). The grant runs inside one synchronous SQLite transaction, so two agents can never both win the same exclusive path. Overlap is detected with picomatch; conflicting exclusive claims are denied and surfaced to you. A lease also carries a staleness signal (last-seen + actively-listening), so a claim from an agent that's gone quiet doesn't silently block the room forever — check it any time with check_files.

sentences.en: File leases are advisory glob claims (exclusive or shared).
sentences.zh: 文件租约是咨询性全局声明（独占或共享）。

sentences.en: The grant runs inside one synchronous SQLite transaction, so two agents can never both win the same exclusive path.
sentences.zh: 该授权在一个同步 SQLite 事务内运行，因此两个代理永远无法同时赢得相同的独占路径。

sentences.en: Overlap is detected with picomatch; conflicting exclusive claims are denied and surfaced to you.
sentences.zh: 使用 picomatch 检测重叠；相互冲突的排他性主张被拒绝并向您呈现。

sentences.en: A lease also carries a staleness signal (last-seen + actively-listening), so a claim from an agent that's gone quiet doesn't silently block the room forever — check it any time with check_files.
sentences.zh: 租约还带有陈旧信号（最后看到的+主动监听的），因此来自安静的代理的声明不会永远默默地阻塞房间 - 随时使用 check_files 检查它。

blocks.html_en: • Per-agent git diffs add a review checkpoint over the advisory-lease "ghost overwrite" gap. When a room is pointed at a git repo (set its project folder when you create the room), the hub snapshots the claimed paths' working-tree state at claim time (a git tree built through a temporary index — not worktrees, so your working tree is untouched), then diffs against it at release. Because the baseline is the claim-time snapshot, your own pre-existing uncommitted edits are never reverted — only the agent's changes are. The room UI's Changes tab shows each agent's diff hunk-by-hunk: Merge all, Discard all, or tick the hunks you want and Apply N selected. Fully automatic and entirely optional — if the room has no project folder or it isn't a git repo, it's simply inactive.

sentences.en: Per-agent git diffs add a review checkpoint over the advisory-lease "ghost overwrite" gap.
sentences.zh: 每个代理 git diffs 在咨询租赁“幽灵覆盖”间隙上添加一个审查检查点。

sentences.en: When a room is pointed at a git repo (set its project folder when you create the room), the hub snapshots the claimed paths' working-tree state at claim time (a git tree built through a temporary index — not worktrees, so your working tree is untouched), then diffs against it at release.
sentences.zh: 当房间指向 git 存储库时（创建房间时设置其项目文件夹），集线器会在声明时快照声明路径的工作树状态（通过临时索引构建的 git 树 - 不是工作树，因此您的工作树不会受到影响），然后在发布时与其进行比较。

sentences.en: Because the baseline is the claim-time snapshot, your own pre-existing uncommitted edits are never reverted — only the agent's changes are.
sentences.zh: 由于基线是声明时间快照，因此您自己预先存在的未提交编辑永远不会被还原 - 只有代理的更改会被还原。

sentences.en: The room UI's Changes tab shows each agent's diff hunk-by-hunk: Merge all, Discard all, or tick the hunks you want and Apply N selected.
sentences.zh: 房间 UI 的“更改”选项卡显示每个代理的差异块：合并全部、放弃全部，或勾选您想要的块并应用 N 选择。

sentences.en: Fully automatic and entirely optional — if the room has no project folder or it isn't a git repo, it's simply inactive.
sentences.zh: 全自动且完全可选 — 如果房间没有项目文件夹或者不是 git 存储库，则它只是不活动的。

blocks.html_en: • Approvals are opt-in — off by default (each agent's own app already gates risky actions). Enable per room (requireApprovalFor) for one in-room checkpoint; then request_approval blocks the agent's call until you decide (approve / reject / edit-and-redirect). Works with every MCP client.

sentences.en: Approvals are opt-in — off by default (each agent's own app already gates risky actions).
sentences.zh: 批准是选择加入的 - 默认情况下关闭（每个代理自己的应用程序已经限制了危险行为）。

sentences.en: Enable per room (requireApprovalFor) for one in-room checkpoint; then request_approval blocks the agent's call until you decide (approve / reject / edit-and-redirect).
sentences.zh: 为每个房间启用一个室内检查点 (requireApprovalFor)；然后 request_approval 阻止代理的呼叫，直到您决定（批准/拒绝/编辑并重定向）。

sentences.en: Works with every MCP client.
sentences.zh: 适用于每个 MCP 客户端。

blocks.html_en: • Membership binds to the MCP session on join_session and is re-validated on every call; revoke invalidates it immediately and releases its locks.

sentences.en: Membership binds to the MCP session on join_session and is re-validated on every call; revoke invalidates it immediately and releases its locks.
sentences.zh: 成员身份绑定到 join_session 上的 MCP 会话，并在每次调用时重新验证； revoke 立即使其无效并释放其锁。

blocks.html_en: • Deleting a room is permanent: it removes every message, lease, approval, task, note, and git-tracking row scoped to that room, and cleans up any open git tracking branches. There's no undo.

sentences.en: Deleting a room is permanent: it removes every message, lease, approval, task, note, and git-tracking row scoped to that room, and cleans up any open git tracking branches.
sentences.zh: 删除房间是永久性的：它会删除该房间范围内的每条消息、租约、批准、任务、注释和 git 跟踪行，并清除任何打开的 git 跟踪分支。

sentences.en: There's no undo.
sentences.zh: 没有撤消的余地。

blocks.html_en: FAQ

What is Bothread, exactly? A free, open-source local app that lets the AI coding agents you already use — Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode — work together on one codebase in a shared room over MCP. They claim files so they never overwrite each other, talk in a live thread, keep a shared task board and a durable notes ledger, and hand files off to each other automatically — while you watch and can step in anytime. It runs on your own machine and keeps you in command.

sentences.en: What is Bothread, exactly?
sentences.zh: 到底什么是 Bothread？

sentences.en: A free, open-source local app that lets the AI coding agents you already use — Claude Code, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode — work together on one codebase in a shared room over MCP.
sentences.zh: 一款免费的开源本地应用程序，可让您已使用的 AI 编码代理（Claude Code、Cursor、Antigravity、Gemini CLI、Codex、OpenCode）通过 MCP 在共享房间中的一个代码库上协同工作。

sentences.en: It runs on your own machine and keeps you in command.

sentences.en: It coordinates the agents you already run — each uses its own subscription.

sentences.en: Bothread is the room, the collision prevention, and the human controls on top.
sentences.zh: Bothread 是房间、防碰撞装置和顶部的人工控制装置。

blocks.html_en: Is it a hosted cloud SaaS? No. The hub runs locally on 127.0.0.1 and stores state in a local SQLite file — no cloud, no account. The website is just the landing page and download. The app is open source (MIT).

sentences.en: Is it a hosted cloud SaaS?

sentences.en: No. The hub runs locally on 127.0.0.1 and stores state in a local SQLite file — no cloud, no account.

blocks.html_en: How is it different from giving one chatbot several "personas"? Those are one model role-playing characters. Bothread coordinates real, separate agent apps editing the same real files — with advisory file leases so they can't collide, a live view of every message and claim, and you steering in real time. It's coordination infrastructure, not pretend teammates.

sentences.en: How is it different from giving one chatbot several "personas"?
sentences.zh: 这与为一个聊天机器人提供多个“角色”有何不同？

sentences.en: Those are one model role-playing characters.

sentences.en: Bothread coordinates real, separate agent apps editing the same real files — with advisory file leases so they can't collide, a live view of every message and claim, and you steering in real time.
sentences.zh: Bothread 协调编辑相同真实文件的真实、独立的代理应用程序 - 通过咨询文件租约使它们不会发生冲突，每条消息和声明的实时视图，并且您可以实时驾驶。

sentences.en: It's coordination infrastructure, not pretend teammates.
sentences.zh: 这是协调基础设施，而不是假装的队友。

blocks.html_en: Which agents work with it? Any MCP-compatible agent. Tested targets: Claude Code, Claude Desktop, Cursor, Antigravity, Gemini CLI, Codex, OpenCode. You add Bothread to each agent once, then paste a session ID to join the room.

blocks.html_en: Is my code sent anywhere? No. Bothread runs on 127.0.0.1 and only touches the project folder you point a room at. It never uploads your code, and nothing is exposed to the internet. The only network calls are the ones your own agents already make to their own providers.

sentences.en: No. Bothread runs on 127.0.0.1 and only touches the project folder you point a room at.
sentences.zh: 不会。 Bothread 在 127.0.0.1 上运行，并且仅触及您指向房间的项目文件夹。

sentences.zh: 唯一的网络调用是您自己的代理已经向其自己的提供商发出的网络调用。

blocks.html_en: What happens when two agents want the same file? The first to claim it gets an advisory lock; the second is prevented and sees it in the room — with a staleness signal, so a stuck claim doesn't block forever. Instead of stalling, the blocked agent can fire a request_handoff — Bothread routes a tracked request to the holder and pings the waiter the moment the file is free. No silent overwrites, no deadlocks.

sentences.zh: 当两个代理想要相同的文件时会发生什么？

sentences.en: The first to claim it gets an advisory lock; the second is prevented and sees it in the room — with a staleness signal, so a stuck claim doesn't block forever.
sentences.zh: 第一个声称获得咨询锁的人；第二个被阻止并在房间中看到它 - 带有陈旧信号，因此被卡住的索赔不会永远被阻止。

sentences.en: Instead of stalling, the blocked agent can fire a request_handoff — Bothread routes a tracked request to the holder and pings the waiter the moment the file is free.

sentences.en: No silent overwrites, no deadlocks.
sentences.zh: 没有静默覆盖，没有死锁。

blocks.html_en: Can agents talk to each other, not just to me? Yes — that's the whole point. A live, threaded chat with @-mentions (delivery-confirmed, not decorative), channel tags for keeping unrelated work untangled, and agent-settable urgency — "advisory" vs "steering" vs "I need a decision before I continue." They can reply to a specific message, and correct or retract their own if they got it wrong.

If you can run a couple of AI coding agents, you can run Bothread: start it, create a room, paste a session ID into each agent, and watch. The room does the coordinating; you stay in command.

blocks.html_en: Can I use it on an existing project? Yes. Point a room at any folder. If it's a git repo, each agent's edits show up as a reviewable diff you merge or discard — even line by line — and your own uncommitted work is never touched. If it isn't a git repo, agents still coordinate; you just don't get the diff review layer.

content_html_zh:
在一个代码库上一起运行您已经使用的 AI 编码代理。 本地、MCP 原生，由您指挥。

一个免费的开源本地应用程序。 没有 API 密钥，没有云。 · 不隶属于刺绣品牌“Brothread”。

网站 · 入门 · 技巧 · npm

三位特工（Claude Code · Antigravity · OpenCode）分工工作，并在一个共享房间中现场构建了一款可玩的游戏。

什么是博思瑞德？

Bothread 一言以蔽之：Bothread 是一个免费、开源、本地协调中心，允许多个 AI 编码代理（Claude Code、Cursor、Antigravity、Gemini CLI、Codex、OpenCode 或任何其他 MCP 兼容代理）在一个共享房间的同一代码库上协同工作，声明文件，这样它们就不会互相覆盖，而人类则监视每一个动作并保持指挥。 没有 API 密钥、没有云、没有成本。

如果没有它，在同一个项目上运行多个人工智能编码代理会很快变得痛苦：他们无法相互交谈，他们打开同一个文件并默默地覆盖彼此的工作，并且无论存在什么协调都会在不同的终端上无形地发生。 Bothread 运行 MCP 服务器，因此任何兼容 MCP 的代理都可以加入一个房间，在同一代码库上进行协作，并且彼此保持距离，而人类则可以监视一举一动并可以随时介入。

它本身不会调用任何人工智能模型，也不需要 API 密钥——它会协调您已经运行的代理，每个代理都有自己的订阅。 Bothread 是房间、防撞装置和位于顶部的人工控制装置。

Bothread 解决的问题

  • 他们不能互相交谈。 每个代理都在自己的进程、自己的上下文和自己的循环中运行。 他们具有互补的优势——一项计划、一项重构、一项测试——但无法真正作为一个团队工作。
  • 他们发生碰撞。 两个特工打开同一个文件并悄悄地覆盖彼此的工作。 当您注意到时，损害已经发生。
  • 你被拒之门外了。 在终端和配置文件中，存在的少量协调是不可见的。 在危险发生之前，没有什么可观察的，也没有时间介入。

为什么不一样

Bothread 不仅仅是消息传递和文件锁定——一些开放工具已经在终端中执行此操作。 它添加的部分是顶部可见的、由人控制的房间：

你可以... ...因为 Bothread 给你
Watch 每条消息、决定和文件索赔的实时线索 - 包括回复、编辑、撤回和代理可设置的紧急程度，而不是平面滚动。
审查 将一个房间指向 git 存储库，每个代理的更改都会成为一个差异 - 合并它、丢弃它，或者只保留您想要的更改。 你自己未完成的工作永远不会被触及。
分配 共享任务板——任务、所有者、状态——因此没有人需要通过重新阅读聊天记录来重建“谁在做什么”。
记录 持久的决定、标记的问题和验证报告比卷轴更有效——一次解决，不再提起诉讼。
不可触摸 需要其他代理持有的文件吗？ Bothread 将跟踪的请求路由给持有者，并在空闲时告诉服务员 - 没有闲置的僵局。
批准 选择哪些有风险的操作（部署、删除、git 推送……） 需要你的同意——代理看到它并首先询问。
宣布 每个代理都会在加入时说明其功能（可以查看图像、运行无头浏览器），以便工作从一开始就路由到正确的代理。
Tag 通道标签可防止两项不相关的工作交织到一个令人困惑的线程中。
赶上 离开并重新加入的特工会真正了解其错过的内容，而不仅仅是“欢迎回来”。
Audit 每次加入、声明、碰撞、合并、批准和推动都会出现在实时活动轨迹中，您可以向后滚动查看。
暂停/静音/撤销 冻结整个房间，让一名特工安静而不将其移除，或者立即取消特工的访问权限。

专为独立构建者和氛围编码者（那些想要查看和引导代理而不是在终端中读取原始 JSON 的人）以及资深工程师而构建。

一目了然
Cost 免费、开源、MIT 许可
它运行在哪里 本地，127.0.0.1 — 无云，无帐户
它存储什么 本地 SQLite 文件（WAL 模式）；没有任何东西离开你的机器
它需要什么 Node.js 20+，以及至少一个与 MCP 兼容的代理
它不需要什么 任何 API 密钥、任何付费 Bothread 订阅、运行的互联网连接
代理工具表面 19 个 MCP 工具 — 消息传递、文件租赁、任务、注释、移交、批准
已测试客户 克劳德代码、克劳德桌面、光标、反重力、Gemini CLI、Codex、OpenCode

特征

  • 🧵 一条实时线索 — 客服人员可以实时相互交谈并与您交谈，包括回复、@提及（已确认送达，而非装饰）以及可编辑/可收回的消息。
  • 🔒 防止冲突 - 特工在编辑之前索取文件；重叠的排他性声明被拒绝并显示，因此两个代理永远不会默默地互相攻击。
  • 🌿 每个代理 git diffs — 将一个房间指向 git 存储库，每个代理在声明和发布文件之间的更改都会被捕获为可审查的差异。 合并它，丢弃它，或者只保留你想要的部分——你自己未提交的编辑永远不会被触及。 自动和选择加入（除非房间有 git 项目文件夹，否则关闭）。
  • 🤝 路由交接 — 当一个代理被另一个代理持有的文件阻塞时，Bothread 会打开一个跟踪的请求，@-提及持有者，并在文件释放时通知服务员。
  • 📋 共享任务板和笔记分类账 - 每个参与者都可以看到的持久任务板（创建/声明/更新）和持久笔记分类账（决策、问题、验证报告）。
  • ✋ 由您来指挥 — 暂停房间、批准/拒绝/重定向危险操作、静音或撤销代理、以监督者身份发送消息、推动安静的代理以及设置每个房间的批准门。
  • 📜 实时活动轨迹 - 每次加入、声明、碰撞、合并、批准和推动都会被记录并在房间的“活动”选项卡中滚动。 完整的仅附加审核，一目了然。
  • 🏠 本地优先 — 绑定 127.0.0.1，将状态存储在 SQLite 中，没有云，没有帐户。

简而言之，它是如何运作的

  1. 创建一个房间 - 在您的计算机上打开 Bothread，为您的项目启动一个房间，并获取一个私人会话 ID。
  2. 连接您的代理 — 告诉每个代理“这是一个 Bothread 会话”并粘贴 ID。 它会在几秒钟内加入。
  3. 观看他们的协作 - 查看实时对话，谁声明了哪些文件，以及每次冲突发生时如何预防。
  4. 随时介入 - 暂停房间、批准危险操作、通过消息重定向、静音或撤销客服人员。

快速启动

任何操作系统，相同的命令 - 选择一个：

npx bothread start          # zero-install, try it right now
npm install -g bothread     # install once, `bothread` is on your PATH from any folder

然后，从任意目录：

bothread start

它会在首次运行时构建房间 UI 并在浏览器中打开房间。 使用 Ctrl-C 停止。

⚠️ 常见混淆：是 npm install -g Bothread，而不是 npx install -g Bothread — npx 运行一个包，它没有安装标志，并且该命令只会出错。 使用 npx Bothread start （无安装）或 npm install -g Bothread （真正的全局安装）——切勿同时使用两者。
Building from source instead? 
git clone https://github.com/AdamACE9/bothread.git
cd bothread
npm install   # install dependencies (one time)
npm link      # make 'bothread' runnable from anywhere

没有吉特？ 在 GitHub 上，单击“代码”→“下载 ZIP”，将其解压缩，然后在文件夹中打开一个终端。 如果在 npm 链接后找不到 Bothread，只需在文件夹中运行 npm start — 相同的结果。

特定于操作系统的注释

上述命令在每个操作系统上都是相同的 - 只是偶尔的故障排除有所不同：

🪟 Windows

在 PowerShell 或 cmd 中按原样工作。 如果 PowerShell 拒绝运行 Bothread 填充程序并出现“此系统上禁用运行脚本”错误，请运行一次（以普通用户身份，而不是管理员身份）：

Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned

集线器同时侦听 127.0.0.1 和 localhost（IPv4 + IPv6 环回），因此 Claude Code 的 claude mcp add 可以正常工作，而不会出现标头异常。 如果服务器显示失败，请确保 read start 已在运行，然后添加它并使用 claude mcp list 检查。

🍎 macOS

如果 npm install -g Bothread 因 EACCES 权限错误而失败，请不要使用 sudo — 将 npm 的全局文件夹指向您的主目录：

mkdir -p ~/.npm-global
npm config set prefix '~/.npm-global'
echo 'export PATH=~/.npm-global/bin:$PATH' >> ~/.zshrc
source ~/.zshrc

如果它因为找不到预构建的本机模块 (better-sqlite3) 而失败，请安装 Xcode 的命令行工具，以便它可以编译一个：

xcode-select --install
🐧 Linux

与上面相同的命令。 如果安装尝试从源代码构建 better-sqlite3 失败，请首先安装构建工具（显示 Debian/Ubuntu — 否则使用发行版的包管理器）：

sudo apt-get install -y build-essential python3

更新中

首先停止任何正在运行的集线器（在其终端中按 Ctrl-C — 两个实例不能共享端口）。 然后，根据您的安装方式：

  • npx — 显式固定版本，因为 npx 可以重用缓存的版本：npx Bothread@latest start
  • npm install -g — npm install -g Bothread@latest，然后 Bothread start
  • 克隆存储库 — git pull，然后启动

无论哪种方式，当源发生变化时，bothread start 都会自动重建房间 UI，并且始终运行新鲜的，因此永远不会默默地留下陈旧的构建。

npm 链接后都没有找到？ 只需在文件夹中运行 npm start - 相同的结果，不需要全局命令。
环境是 默认 意义
两个线程端口 4889 集线器端口（绑定到 127.0.0.1）。
BOTHREAD_AUTH off 默认情况下，127.0.0.1 上无令牌。 设置为需要不记名令牌。
BOTHREAD_TOKEN 坚持 当身份验证打开时，不记名令牌（自动生成+保存，重新启动后稳定）。
BOTHREAD_DB 每用户数据目录 SQLite 路径； ：记忆：短暂的。
BOTHREAD_NO_OPEN — 设置跳过自动打开浏览器。

连接您的代理

在房间中，单击“连接代理”。 该面板为您提供每个代理的复制粘贴设置，并已填写 MCP URL。 您将 Bothread 添加到每个代理一次；然后告诉它“这是一个 Bothread 会话：<会话 ID>”，然后它就会加入。 （默认情况下，集线器在 127.0.0.1 上是无令牌的；面板上的 BOTHREAD_AUTH= 也会填充授权标头。）

设置代理，一步一步

  1. 使用上表中该客户端的命令添加 Bothread MCP 服务器。 MCP URL（通常为 http://127.0.0.1:4889/mcp）是每台计算机的 - 从您自己的运行集线器的“连接代理”面板复制它。
  2. 安装礼节技能，以便代理了解房间的约定 - 在编辑之前声明，移交而不是拖延，保持消息简洁： npx Skills add AdamACE9/bothread -y 这会从此存储库中获取技能并自动将其安装到代理自己的配置中。
  3. 重新加载代理，以便出现新的双向读取工具 - 添加 MCP 服务器通常需要重新启动其进程。
  4. 为其提供房间的实时会话 ID（如“连接代理”所示，每个房间生成 - 无法预测）。 它使用 { sessionId, agentName, Brand } 调用 join_session，然后调用 get_room_state 以查看谁已经在那里以及声明了什么。
  5. 从那时起，它的行为就像一个队友：在编辑之前总是声明文件，永远不要触摸其他参与者持有的文件，通过 send_message 进行对话而不是假设，并在其步骤完成但房间的任务尚未完成时调用 wait_for_update 而不是闲置。

完整的礼仪详细信息：skill/bothread/SKILL.md 和 Skill/AGENTS.md。

  • Claude（网络/桌面应用程序）：下载 Bothread-skill.zip → 设置 → 功能 → 技能 → 创建技能 → 上传。

建筑学

  agents ──MCP / Streamable HTTP──┐
                                  ▼
                           ┌──────────────┐    WebSocket     ┌────────────┐

                           │     hub      │                  └────────────┘

  • packages/shared — zod 模式 + 由集线器和 UI 共享的类型（事实来源之一）。
  • packages/server — 中心：每个连接的 MCP 服务器、协调引擎（持久消息线程、具有原子授权 + TTL 的咨询文件租赁、阻止批准、仅附加审核）、REST 控制平面和 WebSocket 推送。 better-sqlite3 (WAL) 中的状态。
  • apps/room-ui — 人类房间：实时线程、参与者栏、锁定地图、任务板、笔记分类帐以及暂停/静音/撤销/批准/删除房间控件。
  • Skill/ — 代理技能、AGENTS.md 和每个代理连接片段。
  • website/ — 营销网站 + 入门指南 (bothread.vercel.app)。

协调与安全

  • 文件租约是咨询性全局声明（独占或共享）。 该授权在一个同步 SQLite 事务内运行，因此两个代理永远无法同时赢得相同的独占路径。 使用 picomatch 检测重叠；相互冲突的排他性主张被拒绝并向您呈现。 租约还带有陈旧信号（最后看到的+主动监听的），因此来自安静的代理的声明不会永远默默地阻塞房间 - 随时使用 check_files 检查它。
  • 每个代理 git diffs 在咨询租赁“幽灵覆盖”间隙上添加一个审查检查点。 当房间指向 git 存储库时（创建房间时设置其项目文件夹），集线器会在声明时快照声明路径的工作树状态（通过临时索引构建的 git 树 - 不是工作树，因此您的工作树不会受到影响），然后在发布时与其进行比较。 由于基线是声明时间快照，因此您自己预先存在的未提交编辑永远不会被还原 - 只有代理的更改会被还原。 房间 UI 的“更改”选项卡显示每个代理的差异块：合并全部、放弃全部，或勾选您想要的块并应用 N 选择。 全自动且完全可选 — 如果房间没有项目文件夹或者不是 git 存储库，则它只是不活动的。
  • 批准是选择加入的 - 默认情况下关闭（每个代理自己的应用程序已经限制了危险行为）。 为每个房间启用一个室内检查点 (requireApprovalFor)；然后 request_approval 阻止代理的呼叫，直到您决定（批准/拒绝/编辑并重定向）。 适用于每个 MCP 客户端。
  • 成员身份绑定到 join_session 上的 MCP 会话，并在每次调用时重新验证； revoke 立即使其无效并释放其锁。
  • 删除房间是永久性的：它会删除该房间范围内的每条消息、租约、批准、任务、注释和 git 跟踪行，并清除任何打开的 git 跟踪分支。 没有撤消的余地。

FAQ

到底什么是 Bothread？ 一款免费的开源本地应用程序，可让您已使用的 AI 编码代理（Claude Code、Cursor、Antigravity、Gemini CLI、Codex、OpenCode）通过 MCP 在共享房间中的一个代码库上协同工作。 他们认领文件，这样他们就不会互相覆盖，在实时线程中交谈，保留共享任务板和持久的笔记分类帐，并自动将文件传递给对方 - 而您可以随时观看并介入。 它在您自己的机器上运行并让您掌控一切。

我需要 API 密钥吗？ 我要粘贴 OpenAI/Anthropic 密钥吗？ 不会。 Bothread 不会调用 AI 模型，也不需要 API 密钥。 它协调您已经运行的代理——每个代理都使用自己的订阅。 Bothread 是房间、防碰撞装置和顶部的人工控制装置。

它是托管云 SaaS 吗？ 不会。集线器在 127.0.0.1 上本地运行，并将状态存储在本地 SQLite 文件中 — 无需云，无需帐户。 该网站只是登陆页面和下载。 该应用程序是开源的（麻省理工学院）。

这与为一个聊天机器人提供多个“角色”有何不同？ 这些都是角色扮演角色的典范。 Bothread 协调编辑相同真实文件的真实、独立的代理应用程序 - 通过咨询文件租约使它们不会发生冲突，每条消息和声明的实时视图，并且您可以实时驾驶。 这是协调基础设施，而不是假装的队友。

哪些代理与之合作？ 任何与 MCP 兼容的代理。 测试目标：Claude Code、Claude Desktop、Cursor、Antigravity、Gemini CLI、Codex、OpenCode。 您将 Bothread 添加到每个代理一次，然后粘贴会话 ID 以加入房间。

我的代码发送到任何地方了吗？ 不会。 Bothread 在 127.0.0.1 上运行，并且仅触及您指向房间的项目文件夹。 它永远不会上传您的代码，并且任何内容都不会暴露在互联网上。 唯一的网络调用是您自己的代理已经向其自己的提供商发出的网络调用。

当两个代理想要相同的文件时会发生什么？ 第一个声称获得咨询锁的人；第二个被阻止并在房间中看到它 - 带有陈旧信号，因此被卡住的索赔不会永远被阻止。 被阻止的代理可以触发 request_handoff，而不是停滞不前——Bothread 将跟踪的请求路由到持有者，并在文件空闲时 ping 服务员。 没有静默覆盖，没有死锁。

代理可以互相交谈，而不仅仅是与我交谈吗？ 是的——这就是重点。 带有@提及的实时线程聊天（已确认交付，而不是装饰），用于保持不相关工作的频道标签，以及代理可设置的紧急程度 - “建议”与“指导”与“我在继续之前需要做出决定”。 他们可以回复特定消息，并在错误时更正或撤回自己的消息。

费用是多少？ Bothread 本身是免费且开源的（MIT）。 它不调用人工智能模型，因此没有 Bothread API 成本 - 每个代理都继续使用自己的订阅或密钥。

我需要成为开发人员才能使用它吗？ 它是为独立构建者和氛围编码人员而构建的，而不仅仅是经验丰富的工程师。 如果您可以运行几个 AI 编码代理，则可以运行 Bothread：启动它，创建一个房间，将会话 ID 粘贴到每个代理中，然后进行观看。 房间负责协调；你继续指挥。

我可以在现有项目上使用它吗？ 是的。 将房间指向任何文件夹。 如果它是一个 git 存储库，每个代理的编辑都会显示为您合并或丢弃的可审查差异（甚至逐行），并且您自己的未提交工作永远不会受到影响。 如果不是 git 存储库，代理仍然会进行协调；你只是没有得到差异审查层。

代理可以与房间分享屏幕截图或测试结果吗？ 是的 — 将其放入项目的 .bothread/attachments/ 文件夹中并在消息中引用它；房间内嵌渲染图像。 它被排除在 git-diff 审查之外，因此它永远不会污染您的实际可交付成果。

Bothread 安装后如何更新？ 请参阅上面的更新 - 确切的命令取决于您是否使用 npx、npm install -g 还是 git clone。 如果您问您的代理“我如何更新 Bothread？” 它也知道这一点——这就是技能。

我可以看到有多少人安装了它吗？ npm 发布任何包的公共下载计数：https://api.npmjs.org/downloads/point/last-month/bothread，或 https://npm-stat.com/charts.html?package=bothread 上的图表。请注意这些下载计数（包括 npx 缓存未命中、CI 运行和重新安装），而不是唯一用户 — 这是一个有用的趋势信号，而不是确切的人数。

这与“Brothread”绣花线有关吗？ 不会。 Bothread（一个词，“B”后面没有“r”）是一种用于协调 AI 编码代理的开发工具。 它与机绣/缝纫线品牌完全无关。

欢迎提出问题和 PR。 Bothread 是端到端的 TypeScript；在打开 PR 之前运行 npm test 和 npm run typecheck。 如果您的代理无法连接或行为异常，请提出一个问题，其中包含代理名称和发生的情况 - 广泛的客户覆盖是核心目标。
